_comment: Agent-authored §3.2.1 narrative-prose pass over the GIIGNL 2026 Annual Report (liquefaction narrative pp.28-31, regasification narrative pp.48-52). Every forward-looking / status finding from the prose, triaged against the fresh GEM export and (for actionable items) web-verified against authoritative sources. These route to giignl_to_action with their target route. GIIGNL prose is NOT authoritative and is never auto-applied (SOP §3.8); each actionable item carries a verified non-GIIGNL corroborating source. Confidence: high = corroborated by primary/IR or 2+ independent sources; medium = single-origin detail or recent/contested. action_category drives the giignl_to_action routing.

findings.prose_finding: Force majeure (declared 2021) lifted; TotalEnergies announced full restart of onshore+offshore activities. 12.8 MTPA, ~40% complete, first LNG targeted 2029.
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~29; corroborated: TotalEnergies press release 2026-01-29 (full restart); Bloomberg 2025-10-25 (FM lift decision); JPT/SPE.

findings.prose_finding: Bayu-Undan depleted; Barossa feedgas (via Barossa FPSO) now supplying the plant; resumed exports, first Barossa cargo loaded (vessel Kool Blizzard) 25 Jan 2026. 3.7 MTPA single train.
findings.recommended_workflow: Update (+ status_timeline: operating 2026-01) — also explains the giignl_diff Darwin 3.7-vs-0.0 gap (GEM operating total was 0 while idled)
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~30; corroborated: Santos 'first Barossa LNG cargo' (2026-01-27); Rigzone; Argus.

findings.prose_finding: Nguya FLNG shipped first cargo 7 Feb 2026, starting Phase 2; brings Congo LNG complex to ~3 MTPA (with existing Tango FLNG). Gas from Marine XII (Nene/Litchendjili).
findings.recommended_workflow: Update (+ status_timeline: operating 2026-02). FSRU/FLNG vessel-sync touchpoint (Nguya FLNG vessel identity owned by carrier project).
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~28; corroborated: Eni press release 2026-02-07; Oil & Gas Journal; LNG Industry.

findings.gem_terminal_name: QatarEnergy LNG (N) and/or QatarEnergy LNG (S) — identify which units are T4/T6
findings.prose_finding: Iranian missile strikes on 18 Mar 2026 damaged LNG Trains 4 and 6 (~12.8 MTPA, ~17% of Qatar LNG); QatarEnergy declared force majeure; repairs est. 3-5 years. (Post-dates the GIIGNL operating table snapshot, so NOT a giignl_diff correction — a forward Update.)
findings.recommended_status_change: operating -> idled (Trains 4 & 6 only; rest of complex unaffected)
findings.recommended_workflow: Update (+ status_timeline: idled 2026-03). VERIFY which GEM units map to GIIGNL 'Train 4/6'. Hold pending damage assessment.
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~28-29; corroborated: Al Jazeera 2026-03-24 (force majeure); CNBC 2026-03-18; Reuters via Bloomberg. NOTE: precise train numbers (4 & 6) are single-origin (QatarEnergy CEO via Reuters); figures still under assessment.

findings.prose_finding: US sanctioned operator 13 Jan 2025; exports ceased after OFAC grace period ended 27 Feb 2025 (last cargo mid-Jan 2025). ~1.5 MTPA. Plant physically intact.
findings.recommended_status_change: operating -> idled (sanctions-driven; apply lifecycle_rules.md — idled/mothballed, not cancelled)
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~27; corroborated: LNG Prime 2025-01; Reuters via Gas Processing News; Pipeline & Gas Journal.

findings.prose_finding: US sanctioned operator 13 Jan 2025; exports ceased after grace period 27 Feb 2025 (last cargo dispatched 18 Feb 2025 -> Zeebrugge). ~0.82 MTPA.
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~27; corroborated: LNG Prime 2025-01; Reuters via Gas Processing News.

findings.prose_finding: Train 7 >80% complete (GIIGNL); now 92% as of 21 May 2026 (NLNG official), entering pre-commissioning. Raises Bonny capacity 22->30 MTPA (+8).
findings.recommended_status_change: no lifecycle change (remains construction) — progress + anchor-year/confidence update
findings.recommended_workflow: Update (confidence bump; confirm % + no first-LNG date yet)
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~28; corroborated: ThisDay 2026-05-21 (92%); NLNG official Train 7 page; Punch.

findings.site_name: Khor Al Zubair LNG Import Terminal (FSRU)
findings.prose_finding: Iraq's FIRST LNG import terminal. Excelerate Energy signed definitive agreement with Iraq Ministry of Electricity 28 Oct 2025; FSRU 'Hull 3407' (170,000 cbm) under construction at HD Hyundai, delivery 2026, COD 2026. Capacity ~500 MMscf/d guaranteed (vessel up to 1,000 MMscf/d); min offtake 250 MMscf/d. ~$450M.
findings.recommended_status_change: ADD as construction/proposed (agreement signed + FSRU under construction)
findings.recommended_workflow: Discovery (meets 'sufficient info to add': sponsor Excelerate + location Khor Al Zubair + signed agreement). FSRU SYNC: vessel = Hull 3407 (no name/IMO yet) -> cross-check carrier project. Run capacity_normalize on MMscf/d -> MTPA.
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 regas narrative p.51; corroborated: Excelerate Energy press release 2025-10-28.

findings.gem_status_now: NOT IN GEM (distinct from existing 'Coveñas FSRU')
findings.prose_finding: Pacific-coast FSU-based import: FSU/marine at Buenaventura, regas plant at Buga (Valle del Cauca). Owner Regasificadora del Pacifico (RDP, part of PIO SAS); Ecopetrol offtaker; Exmar FSU lessor (5-yr charter, secured Sept 2025). Financial close ~Feb 2026 ($130M senior debt). Ops expected Q3 2026. ~60 MMcf/d (~0.45 MTPA, derived).
findings.recommended_status_change: ADD as construction (financial close reached, FSU conversion underway)
findings.recommended_workflow: Discovery. FSU SYNC: vessel not yet named -> leave blank. entity_lookup: 'Regasificadora del Pacifico' / 'PIO SAS' (likely new). Run capacity_normalize (source gives MMcf/d, not MTPA).
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 regas narrative p.~50; corroborated: Exmar press release (financing close); LNG Prime 2026-02; LNG Industry. NB: Ecopetrol-PIO contract was Feb 2025 (GIIGNL said March).

findings.site_name: Southern Energy (SESA) FLNG — Hilli Episeyo + Golar MK II
findings.gem_terminal_name: Argentina LNG Terminal (existing umbrella; SESA Phase 1 likely missing at unit granularity)
findings.prose_finding: SESA FLNG in Gulf of San Matías, Río Negro. FID taken on TWO FLNG units: Hilli Episeyo (2.45 MTPA, relocating from Cameroon/Kribi ~mid-2027, ops Q4 2027) + Golar MK II (3.5 MTPA, late 2028); combined ~5.95 MTPA. Owners: Pan American Energy 30%, YPF 25%, Pampa Energía 20%, Harbour Energy 15%, Golar LNG 10%.
findings.recommended_status_change: ADD/UPDATE: SESA Phase 1 (Hilli + MK II) as FID'd construction units under the Argentina LNG complex — more advanced lifecycle than the rest of the umbrella
findings.recommended_workflow: Discovery/Update (two new FLNG units). FLNG vessel-sync: Hilli Episeyo is a relocating vessel (currently Cameroon FLNG) -> carrier-project cross-check. Distinguish from the pre-FID YPF/Eni/XRG ARGLNG 12 MTPA phase.
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 liq narrative p.~29; corroborated: Harbour Energy IR (SESA FID); Offshore Mag; Oil & Gas Journal; JPT/SPE.

findings.prose_finding: Hokkaido Gas studying a new carbon-neutral LNG hub (LNG + future H2/e-methane) at Tomakomai East Port. Funded FY2024-FY2025 feasibility study; no FID, no capacity, no target year.
findings.recommended_workflow: Monitor (sponsor + location + feasibility study, but no capacity/FID)
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 regas narrative p.~48; corroborated: LNG Prime ('Hokkaido Gas eyes new LNG terminal').

findings.site_name: ≈50 forward-looking prose mentions already in GEM at the correct lifecycle — confidence bump / no new record
findings.prose_finding: US: LNG Canada T1/2 (op), Corpus Christi Stage 3 (constr/op), Plaquemines Ph1/2 (constr/op), Calcasieu Pass (op since 15 Apr 2025), CP2 (constr, FID Jul 2025), Rio Grande T4/5 (FID, constr), Golden Pass (constr/op T1 first-LNG Mar 2026), Woodside Louisiana/Driftwood (constr, FID Apr 2025), Port Arthur Ph1/2 (constr), Commonwealth (constr), Texas LNG (proposed), Sabine Pass Midscale T8/9 (FID Jun 2025), Magnolia/Delfin/Alaska (proposed). Canada: Woodfibre (constr 65%), Cedar->'Cedar FLNG' (constr), Ksi Lisims (proposed). Russia: Arctic LNG 2 T2 (started May 2025, op/idled), Murmansk (proposed), Obsky LNG (proposed). UAE: Ruwais LNG (constr, FID Jun 2024). Oman: Marsa LNG -> verify GEM has it (FID Apr 2024, 1 MTPA, start 2028). Australia: Gorgon Stage 3 (sanctioned). Indonesia: Abadi (proposed). Mexico: Costa Azul (constr/op, Ph1 95%). Mozambique: Coral North FLNG (proposed)/Coral South (op). Mauritania/Senegal: GTA Tortue (op/proposed, FLNG Gimi ramped to ~3 MTPA). Regas already-tracked: Chhara (op), Dahej expansion (constr), Dabhol (op), Gopalpur (proposed), Crown Kakinada (proposed), Dhamra (op), Kochi (op), Wenzhou Huagang=Zhuangyuanao (op), Hongmei=Dongguan (op), Jieyang(PipeChina)=Yuedong (op+expansion), Shanghai Yangshan (op), Yangjiang (op), Zhoushan (op), Taoyuan=Guantang (op+proposed), Taichung (op/constr/proposed), Port Kembla (constr), Outer Harbor/Geelong/Port Phillip Bay (proposed), Summit Matarbari (proposed), Botum Sakor (proposed), Hibiki (constr/op), Lumut/RGT Yan (proposed), SLNG 2 (proposed), KOGAS Dangjin (constr), NE Asia Hub Yeosu (constr), Boryeong (op), Map Ta Phut 3 (proposed), Thi Vai (op/proposed), Cai Mep (op), Son My (proposed), Mukran/Wilhelmshaven TES/Stade/Brunsbüttel (Germany, op/constr), Gas Networks Ireland (proposed), Coveñas FSRU (proposed), Cartagena FSRU expansion (op/proposed), Manzanillo DR=Pepillo Salcedo (constr), Terminal Gás Sul=TGS (idled), Aqaba Jordan FSRU (op).
findings.recommended_workflow: No new record. Optional: confidence bump + anchor-year refresh where stale. Capacity reconciliation flags noted in qa_review (Wenzhou Huagang, Jieyang, NWS post-T2).
findings.citation: GIIGNL 2026 narrative pp.28-31, 48-52; GEM cross-check 2026-05-29 against fresh export.
